pages.text: 26-26  January 20, 2026  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE APPOINTMENT OF SPECIAL COUNSEL DUE  TO A CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND APPROVING PROFESSIONAL FEES  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )  recognizes   that   circumstances   may   arise   in   which   the   Township   Attorney   is   unable   to   represent  the interests of a Township   O fficial due to an actual or potential conflict of interest; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Code   §2-9.4   authorizes   the   appointment   of   Special   Counsel  when   the   Township   Atto rney   determines   that   a   conflict   exists   or   that   the   interests   of   the  Township   or   its   officials   require   separate   representation,   provided   that   the   Township   Council   is  advised   of   the   proposed   engagement,   the   applicable   rate   of   compensation,   and   a   limitation   on  total fees; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   received   notice   of   allegations   of   a   personnel   matter  concerning   the acts of an official or employee   of the Township in his/her official capacity; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Attorney   has   determined ,   upon   initial   r eview   of   the  allegations,   that   a   conflict   of   interest   exists   which   precludes   representation   by   the   Township  Attorney   in   connection   with   the   investigation ,   that   must   remain   confidential,   of   allegations   of   a  personnel   matter   concerning   the   acts   of   an   official   or   employee   of   the   Township   in   his/her  official capacity ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Attorney   has   recommended   the   appointment   of   outside  counsel   Deena   B.   Rosendahl,   Esq.,   of   Cle a ry,   Giacobbe,   Alfieri,   Jacobs,   LLC,   to   serve   as   Special  Counsel   ( “ Special   Cou nsel ” )   for   the   limited   purpose   of   conducting   a   confidential   investigat ion   of  the   allegations   of   a   personnel   matter   concerning   the   acts   of   an   official   or   employee   of   the  Township in his/her official capacity ; and  WHEREAS ,   in   accordance   with   and   pursuant   to   §   2-9.4   the   Township   Attorney   has  designated   Ramon   E.   Rivera,   Esq.,   of   Antonelli,   Kantor,   Rivera,   P.C.,   as   the   Township  Attorney ’ s designated Conflict Counsel to supervise the   work of Special Counsel; and

2  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   has   been   advised   of   the   identity   of   the   proposed  Special   Counsel,   the   scope   of   representation,   the   hourly   rate   of   compensation,   and   the   maximum  amount   to   be   expended   for   such   services,   in   accordance   with   the   requirements   of   the   Township  Code; and  WHEREAS ,   the   proposed   Special   Counsel   shall   be   compensated   at   a   rate   of   $185.00   per  hour ,   and   the   total   compensation   for   such   services   shall   not   exceed   $7,500.00 ,   without   further  encumbrance   unless further authorized by formal action of the Township Council; and  WHEREAS ,   sufficient   f unds   have   been   certified   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   to   cover  the authorized expenditure.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of West Orange, in the County of Essex and State of New Jersey, as follows:  1.   Authorization   of   Special   Counsel.   The   Township   Attorney   is   hereby   authorized,  pursuant   to   §   2-9.4   of   the   Township   Code,   to   appoint   and   retain   outside   legal   counsel   to   serve   as  Special   Counsel   due   to   the   existence   of   a   conflict   of   interest   that   precludes   representation   by   the  Township Attorney.  2.   Appointment   of   Special   Counsel.   The   Township   Attorney   has   conducted   a  preliminary   review   of   the   allegations   and   has   determined   that   a   conflict   exists,   which   precludes  the   Township   Attorney   from   investigating   the   allegations   asserted   against   an   official   or  employee   of   the   Township   in   his/her   official   capacity   and   pursuant   to   §   2-9.4   of   the   Township  Code,   hereby   appoints   Deena   B.   Rosendahl,   Esq.,   of   Clery,   Giacobbe,   Alfieri,   Jacobs,   LLC,   to  serve   as   Special   Counsel   for   the   limited   purpo se   of   investigating   the   allegations   asserted   against  an official or employee of the Township in his/her official capacity .  3.   Appointment   of   Designated   Conflict   Counsel.   The   Township   Attorney   has  determined   that   a   conflict   exists,   which   precludes   the   Township   Attorney   from   investigating   the  allegations   asserted   and   pursuant   to   §   2-9.4   of   the   Township   Code,   hereby   designates   himself,

3  Mark   Semeraro   to   generally   supervise   the   work   of   Special   Counsel ,   Deena   B.   Rosendahl,   Esq.,  of Clery, Giacobbe, Alfieri, Jacobs,   LLC .  4.   Scope   and   Compensation.   Special   Counsel   shall   provide   legal   services   limited   to  the   matter   for   which   the   conflict   exists,   at   an   hourly   rate   of   $185.00 ,   with   total   compensation   not  to   exceed   $7,500.00 ,   without   further   encumbrance   or   authorization   by   resolution   of   the  Township Council.  5.   Township   Council   Oversight   and   Approval.   All   invoices   submitted   by   Special  Counsel   shall   be   subject   to   review   and   approval   in   accordance   with   Township   procedures   and   the  requirements applicable to Special Counsel engageme nts.  6.   Certification   of   Funds.   This   Resolution   shall   take   effect   only   upon   certification  by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   that   sufficient   funds   are   available   and   properly   encumbered   for   the  purposes set forth herein.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   certified   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be  provided   to   the   Township   Attorney,   the   Chief   Financial   Officer,   and   such   other   officials   as   may  be necessary to effectuate its terms.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan Scarpa  Township Clerk   Council Presid ent  Adopted:   January 20, 2026

26 -26  PROFESSIONAL SERVICES AGREEMENT  SPECIAL COUNSEL   –   INVESTIGATIVE SERVICES  This   Professional   Services   Agreement   (the   “ Agreement ” )   is   made   and   entered   into   as   of  January   20 ,   2026   (the   “ Effective   Date ” ),   by   and   between   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   a  municipal   corporation   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   with   offices   located   at   66   Main   Street,   West  Orange,   New   Jersey   07052   (the   “ Township ” ),   and   Deena   B.   Rosendahl,   Esq.,   of   Cleary,  Giacobbe,   Alfieri,   Jacobs,   LLC,   with   offices   located   at   169   Ramapo   Valley   Road   |   U pper   Level  Suite   105,   Oakland,   New   Jersey   07436   ( “ Special   Counsel ”   or   “ Counsel ” )   (collectively   referred  to as the   “ Parties ” ) .  RECITALS  A.   The   Township   has   received   an   allegation   concerning   conduct   attributed   to   a n   official   or  employee   of   the   Township   (the   “ Subject   Official ” )   arising   out   of   acts   undertaken   while  serving in   his/her   official capacity.  B.   The   Township   Attorney   has   conducted   a   preliminary   review   of   the   allegations   and   has  determined   that   an   actual   or   potential   conflict   of   interest   exists   that   preclud es   representation  by the Township Attorney.  C.   Pursuant   to   §2-9.4   of   the   Township   Code,   which   governs   the   appointment   of   Special  Counsel,   and   consistent   with   the   Township ’ s   established   practice s ,   the   Township   Council  has   adopted   Resolution   No.   17-2 026   (the   “ Authorizing   Resolution ” )   approving   the  appointment s   of   Designated   Conflict   Counsel,   Ramon   E.   Rivera,   Esq.,   of   Antonell i ,   Kantor,  Rivera,   P.C. ,   with   offices   located   at   354   Eisenhower   Parkway,   Livingston,   New   Jersey  07039   ( “ Conflict   Counsel ” )   and   Special   Cou nsel,   approving   the   hourly   rate   of  compensation, and establishing a not-to-exceed limitation on fees.  D.   The   Township   desires   to   retain   Special   Counsel   on   a   limited   basis   to   conduct   an   independent  investigation   into   the   aforementioned   allegations   and   to   prepare   a   Final   Report   consisting   of  the Special Counsel ’ s objective findings and   non-binding   recommendations to the Township .

2  NOW,   THEREFORE ,   in   consideration   of   the   mutual   covenants   and   promises   herein,   the  Parties agree as follows:  1.   Engagement & Incorporat ion of Authorizing Resolution .  1.1 .   Engagement.   The   Township   hereby   retains   Special   Counsel,   and   Special   Counsel  accepts such retention,   for the purposes set forth in this Agreement.  1.2 .   Resolution   Controls.   The   parties   acknowledge   and   agree   that   the   Authorizing  Resolution   is   a   material   part   of   this   Agreement   which   is   incorporated   by   reference   and   is   a  condition   precedent   to   payment.   In   the   event   of   a   conflict   or   inconsistency   between   this  Agreement   and   the   Authorizing   Resolution   regarding   compensation   li mits   and   approval  requirements, the Authorizing Resolution shall control.  2.   Term of Appointment.  2.1 .   Term .   This   Agreement   shall   commence   on   the   Effective   Date   and   shall   continue   until  the   earlier   of:   (a)   completion   and   delivery   of   the   Final   Report   to   Conflict   Counsel   (as   defined  below);   (b)   exhaustion   of   the   Not-to-Exceed   Amount;   or   (c)   termination   pursuant   to   Section   1 4  of this Agreement .  2.2 .   No   Annual   Guarantee.   Nothing   herein   shall   be   construed   as   an   appointment   beyond   the  limited matter described in t his Agreement.  3.   Scope of Services.  3.1 .   Purpose.   Special   Counsel   is   retained   solely   to   conduct   an   independent   investigation   into  the   allegations   asserted   against   an   official   or   employee   of   the   Township   that   has   been   identified  in   the   allegations   received   ( the   “ Subject   Official ” )   and   prepare   a   written   Final   Report   regarding  the factual findings, conclusions, and   non-binding   recommendations, if any .  3.2 .   Services .   Special   Counsel   may   perform   the   following   under   the   supervision   of   the  Conflict Counsel , as reas onably necessary and appropriate:

3  a.   Intake   and   Planning.   Review   the   allegation(s),   define   the   investigative   plan,   identify  potential witnesses and sources, and create an evidence preservation / collection protocol.  b.   Document   Requests   and   Review.   Request,   collect,   and   review   relevant   documents,  communications,   recordings,   policies,   and   other   materials,   whether   maintained   by   the  Township, third parties, or the Subject   Official , subject to lawful limits.  c.   Interviews.   Conduct   interviews   of   relevant   witness es,   including   Township   employees,  officials,   and   third   parties,   as   appropriate.   Counsel   shall   determine   interview   sequencing,  interview   format,   and   whether   to   memorialize   interviews   by   notes,   written   statements,   or  other means.  d.   Legal/Policy   Assessment   (Limited).   Evaluate   the   allegations   in   light   of   applicable  Township   policies,   state   law   and   regulations,   and   ethical   standards   as   relevant   to   the  Investigation   (without   providing   individual   legal   defense   representation   to   any   person  unless separately author ized in writing).  3.3 .   Final   Report.   Special   Counsel   shall   prepare   a   written   investigative   report   ( the   “ Final  Report ” )   summarizing   the   factual   findings   and   recommendations.   Any   recommendations  contained   within   the   Final   Report   are   non-binding   upon   the   Township .   Conflict   Counsel   may  request   a   draft   for   comment   prior   to   finalization.   The   Final   Report   shall   be   delivered   to   the  Conflict Counsel   and   the   Township Attorney.  3.4.   Exclusions.   Unless   expressly   authorized   in   writing   by   Conflict   Counsel ,   Special   Counse l  shall not :  a.   Represent   the   Township   or   any   individual   in   litigation,   administrative   proceedings,   or  criminal matters relating to the allegations;  b.   Serve as personal counsel to the subject member of the Township Council ;  c.   Act as Township Attorney, labor counsel, prosecutor, or hearing officer ;

4  d.   Have   subpoena   power   or   compulsory   authority   unless   expressly   authorized   by   law  and authorization of Conflict Counsel ;  e.   Incur   third-party   costs   (i.e.:   investigators,   e-discovery   vendors,   court   reporter)  without prior wri tten authorization   from Conflict Counsel;  f.   Impose   or   recommend   discipline   except   as   part   of   n on-binding   recommendations  contained in the Final Report.  4.   Executive Session Presentation.  Upon   written   request   of   the   Township,   and   to   the   extent   permitted   by   law,   Special   Counsel  along   with   Conflict   Counsel   shall   present   the   findings   of   the   Investigation   and   the   Final   Report  to the Township   Attorney.  5.   No   Individual Client Relationship .  5 . 1 .   Client .   The client for purposes of this Agreement is the Township of West O range.  5.2.   No   Personal   Representation.   Nothing   herein   shall   be   construed   as   creating   an   attorney-  client relationship   between Special Counsel and the Subject   Official   or any other individual.  5 . 3 .   Upjohn   |   Entity   Warnings.   When   interviewing   the   Township   employees   or   officials,  Special   Counsel   may   provide   an   entity   representation   warning   that   Counsel   represents   the  Township and not the individual.  6.   Evidence Preservation & Litigation Hold.  6 . 1.   Coordination .   Upon   request   and   in   conjunction   with   Conflict   Cou nsel,   Special   Counsel  may   coordinate   with   the   Township   Attorney,   Township   Administrator,   and   Township   IT  Department   regarding   document   retention,   evidence   preservation,   and   litigation-hold   measures  relevant to the Investigation.  6.2 .   No   Independent   Authority.   Special   Counsel   shall   not   independently   issue   litigation  holds, subpoenas, or compulsory process without the approval of Conflict Counsel.  7.   Compensation |   Not-to-Exceed Amount .

5  7 . 1 .   Hourly   Rate .   Special   Counsel   shall   be   compensated   at   $185.00   per   hour   for   attorney  time unless otherwise stated in an approved written amendment.  7 .2 .   Not-to-Exceed   Amount.   Total   compensation   under   this   Agreement   shall   not   exceed  $7,500.00   (the   “ Not-to-Exceed   Amount ” )   without   a   further   written   encumbrance   and   additional  authorization by resolution of the Township Council.  7 .3 .   Invoices.   Special   Counsel   shall   submit   itemized   invoices   to   the   Township   on   a   monthly  basis   (or   upon   completion   of   services ,   whichever   occurs   first ),   setting   forth,   at   minimum:   the  date   of   service,   a   description   of   the   work   performed,   the   time   expended,   applicable   billing   rate,  and   the   total amount due.  7 .4 .   Township   Approval .   Payment   of   invoices   is   subject   to   Township   approval   in  accordance   with   applicable   Township   procedures,   including   review   by   Conflict   Counsel   (as  appropriate), CFO, and any other approving authority.  7 .5 .   Costs/Expenses.   Unless   pre-approved   in   writing,   Special   Counsel   shall   not   bill   for  extraordinary   costs   (i . e.:   investigators,   vendors,   transcription,   travel,   copying   in   excess   of  o rdinary   office   practice).   Approved   costs,   if   any,   shall   be   billed   at   the   actual   cost   with   receipts  and shall count toward the Not-to-Exceed Amount unless otherwise authorized in writing.  8.   Confidentiality | Records | OPRA.  8 . 1 .   Confidentiality .   Special   Counsel   shall   treat   all   non-public   information   obtained   in   the  Investigation   as   confidential   to   the   extent   permitted   by   law,   the   Rules   of   Professional   Conduct,  and any lawful directions from the   Township.  8 .2 .   OPRA   Cooperation .   Special   Counsel   acknowledges   the   Township   is   subject   to   OPRA  and   common-law   access   principles.   Special   Counsel   shall   cooperate   with   the   Township   Attorney  or   custodian   of   records   in   responding   to   lawful   requests,   including   identifying   potentially  privileged/confidential material and producing a privilege log if requested .

6  8.3 .   Record   Retention.   Special   Counsel   shall   retain   the   complete   investigative   file   and   shall  return Township property and records upon request, subject to retention obligations.  8 . 4 .   Work   Product   and   Privilege.   To   the   extent   applicable,   Special   Counsel ’ s   work   product  and privileged communications shall be maintained and asserted on behalf of the   Township.  9.   Non-Appropriation.  9 . 1 .   Encumbrance   and   Certification .   This   Agreement   is   contingent   upon   the   certification  of funds by the Chief Financial Officer for the Township and proper encumbrance.  9 .2.   Non-Appropriation.   In   the   event   funds   are   not   appropriated   or   are   otherwise  unavailable,   the   Township   may   terminate   this   Agreement   without   penalty,   and   Special   Counsel  shall   be   paid   only   for   services   rendered   and   approved   of   prior   to   the   effective   date   of  termination, subject to the Not-to-Exceed Amount.  10.   Insurance.  10 . 1 .   Professional   Liability .   During   the   term   of   this   Agreement,   Special   Counsel   shall  maintain   a   professional   liability   (malpractice)   insurance   policy   in   an   amount   not   less   that  $1,000,000   per   claim   and   shall   provide   proof   of   coverage   upon   request,   including   identifying   the  Township as an additional insured.  10 .2 .   Additional   Insurance.   If   Special   Counsel   employes   no n-attorney   personnel   or   uses  subcontractors   in   the   course   of   the   scope   of   this   Agreement,   Special   Counsel   shall   maintain   any  additional insurance reasonably necessary to cover such activities.  11.   Legal Compliance.  Special   Counsel   shall   comply   with   all   applicable   federal   and   New   Jersey   laws,   regulations,  and   ethical   rules,   including   the   New   Jersey   Rules   of   Professional   Conduct ,   pay-to-play   laws,   and  Township policies .  12.   Ownership of Work-Product | Deliverables.

7  1 2 . 1 .   Deliverables .   The   principal   deliverable   is   the   Final   Report ,   delivered   to   Conflict  Counsel   in draft and final form, unless the Township directs otherwise.  1 2 .2 .   Ownership.   Subject   to   applicable   privilege   and   ethical   obligations,   the   Township   shall  own   the   work   product   created   under   this   Agreement,   including   the   Final   Report   and   supporting  materials,   except   that   Special   Counsel   may   retain   a   copy   of   the   file   consistent   with   record  retention obligations and professional responsibility requirements.  13.   Conflicts.  1 3 . 1 .   No   Disqualifying   Conflict .   Special   C ounsel   represents   that,   to   the   best   of   its  knowledge   after   reasonable   diligence,   there   exists   no   conflict   that   would   materially   impair   the  Investigation.   Special   Counsel   shall   promptly   disclose   any   potential   conflict   that   arises   during  the term.  1 3 .2.   Removal   |   Replacement.   Should   a   conflict   arise   that   cannot   be   waived   or   otherwise  managed, the Township may terminate this Agreement and retain a substitute counsel.  14.   Termination.  1 4 . 1 .   Termination   for   Convenience .   The   Township   may   terminate   this   Agreement,   with   or  without   cause,   upon   written   notice   to   Special   Counsel.   Upon   termination,   Special   Counsel   shall  cease   work   (except   to   preserve   the   status   of   the   Investigation   as   requested)   and   submit   a   final  invoice.  1 4 .2 .   Termination   for   Cause.   Either   party   may   terminate   this   Agreement   for   material  breach upon written notice and a reasonable opportunity to cure, where cure is   not   feasible.  1 4 .3 .   Compensation   Upon   Termination.   In   all   events,   Special   Counsel   shall   be  compensated   only   for   approved   work   performed   throu gh   the   effective   date   of   termination ,  subject to the Not-to-Exceed Amount.  15.   Independent Contractor.  Special Counsel is an independent contractor and not an employee of the Township.

8  16.   Notices.  All   formal   notices   required   under   this   Agreement   shall   be   made   in   writing   and   delivered  electronically and by certified mail, return receipt requested addressed as follows:  To the Township:   To Special Counsel:  Township of West Orange   Deena B. Rosendahl, Esq.  Attn.: Karen Carnevale, Twp. Clerk   Cleary, Giacobbe , Alfieri, Jacobs, LLC  66 Main Street   169 Ramapo Valley Road | Upper Level Suite 105  West Orange, New Jersey 07052   Oakland, New Jersey 07436  (973) 325-4155   (973) 845-6700  KCarnevale@westorange.org   Drosendahl@cgajlaw.com  To: Conflict Counsel:  Antonelli, Kantor, Rivera, P.C.  Attn.:   Ramon E. Rivera, Esq.  354 Eisenhower Parkway  Livingston , New Jersey   07039  ( 908) 623-3676  Rrivera@akrlaw.com  17.   Governing Law   and Venue.  This   Agreement   shall   be   governed   by   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey.   Venue   for   any  action   arising   under   this   Agreement   shall   lie   in   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Essex  Vicinage, unless otherwise required by law.  18.   Entire Agreement.  This   Agreement,   together   with   the   Authorizing   Resolution   and   any   required   certifications  incorporated   herein,   constitute   the   entire   agreement   between   the   Parties   and   supersedes   any   prior  agreements.   Any   amendment   hereto   must   be   in   writing,   signed   by   the   Parties   and   where  required,   authorized   by   resolution   of   the   Township   Council   and   accompanied   by   a   proper  encumbrance.  19.   Execution.

9  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF ,   the   Parties   have   executed   this   Agreement   as   of   the   Effective  Date.  Township of West Orange  By:   Title :   Township Attorney  Mark Semeraro, Esq.  Dated:__________________________  Special Counsel :   Cleary, Giacobbe, Alfieri, Jacobs, LLC  By:__________________________   Title:   Special   Counsel  Deena B. Rosendahl, Esq.  Dated:________________________ __

documents.title: AUTH. AN EXTENSION OF BID CONTRACT TO STERLING SECURITIES - MUNICIPAL BLDG.

pages.text: 27-26  January 20, 2026  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE EXTENSION OF THE BID CONTRACT FOR  ARMED/UNARMED SECURITY OFFICERS FOR THE  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE MUNICIPAL BUILDING  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   April   16,   2024,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the  “ Township ” )   issued   an   Invitation   to   Bid   with   the   Township ’ s   Bid   Specifications   seeking  solicitations   for   is   Contract   for   Armed/Unarmed   Security   Officers   (the   “ Contract ” )   for   the  Township of West Orange Municipal Building at 66 Main Street; and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   30,   2024,   a   bid   was   submitted   by   Sterling   Securities,   L.L.C.,  (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   “ Contractor ” ),   seeking   award   of   the   Contract   for   Security   Services  (the   “ Contract ” )   for   a   term   of   one   (1)   year   with   an   option   for   the   Township   to   renew   the   Contract  for   either   an   additional:   (i)   one   (1)   additional   one-year   term;   or   (ii)   two   (2)   additional   one-year  terms   at   an   hourly   rate   of   $35.00   per   hour   armed   security   officer   or   $25.00   per   hour   unarmed  security officer; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   now   wishes   to   extend   the   Contract   for   one   (1)   additional   one-  year term as provided for in the Contract.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL  COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby  authorized   to   execute   an   agreement,   in   the   form   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ” ,   to   extend   the  Contract   for   Armed/Unarmed   Security   Services   for   a   term   of   one   year   with   an   option   to   renew  for an additional year.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   available   in   the   Office  of the Township Clerk in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan Scarpa  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: January 20, 2026  I hereby certify funds are available from   Acc ount No. :  John Ditinyak, Chief Financial Officer

27 -26  AGREEMENT FOR ARMED/UNARMED SECURITY OFFICERS FOR   TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE MUNICIPAL BUILDING  THIS   AGREEMENT ,   dated   as   of   t his   20 th   day   of   JANUARY,   2026 ,   between   the  Township   of   West   Orange,   a   Municipal   Corporation,   in   the   County   of   Essex,   in   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   “ Township ” ),   having   its   principal   offices   as   66   Main   Street,  West   Orange,   New   Jersey;   and   STERLING   SECURITIES,   L.L.C.,   (hereinafter   referred   to   as  the   “ Contractor ” ),   having   its   principal   offices   at   155   Pompton   Avenue,   Verona,   New   Jersey  07044.  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   April   16,   2024,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the  “ Township ” )   issued   an   Invitation   to   Bid   with   the   Township ’ s   Bid   Specifications   seeking  solicitations   for   is   Contract   for   Armed/Unarmed   Security   Officers (the   “ Contract ”) for   the  Township of West Orange Municipal Building at 66 Main Street; and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   30,   2024,   a   bid   was   submitted   by   Sterling   Securities,   L.L.C.,  (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the   “ Contractor ” ),   seeking   award   of   the   Contract   for   Security   Services  (the   “ Contract ” )   for   a   term   of   one   (1)   year   with   an   option   for   the   Township   to   renew   the   Contract  for   either   an   additional:   (i)   one   (1)   additional   one-year   term;   or   (ii)   two   (2)   additional   one-year  terms   at   an   hourly   rate   of   $35.00   per   hour   armed   security   officer   or   $25.00   per   hour   unarmed  security officer; and  WHEREAS ,   on   January   1,   2025,   the   Township   entered   into   an   Agreement   for   Armed  and   Unarmed   Security   Officers   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Municipal   Building   for   a  period of one (1) year; and  WHEREAS , the   Township   now   wishes   to   extend   the   Contract   for   one   (1) additional one-  year term as provided for in the Contract.  WITNESSETH  1.   The   Contract   hereby   covenants   and   agrees   to   furnish   and   deliver   to   the   Township,  Security   Services   consistent   with   the   scope   of   services   set   forth   in   the   Bid   Specifications   and   the  Contractor ’ s   Bid   submitted   on   April   30,   2024   (the   “ Bid ” )   consistent   with   the   Township ’ s  determination   of   the   number   of   armed   officers   and   unarmed   officers   at   the   hourly   rates   set   forth  in   the   Bid,   but   which   cannot   exceed   the   Annual   Cost   for   one   Armed   Security   Officer   at   an   hourly  rate   of   $35.00   per   hour   as   set   forth   in   the   Bi d   for   a   total   of   1960   hours   for   a   year   for   a   total  annual contract price of   $68,600 .  2.   In   the   event   of   any   conflicts   between   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   Bid  Specifications   and   the   Bid,   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   Bid   Specifications   shall  govern.  3.   The   Contractor   acknowledges   that   it   has   read   N.J.S.A.   10:2-1,   et   seq .,  (Discrimination   in   Employment   on   Public   Works;   contract   provision),   the   terms   of   which   are

incorporated   herein   as   if   expressly   set   forth   at   length,   and   the   Contractor   agrees   to   comply   with  and be bound by the terms thereof.  4.   Neither this Agreement nor the monies to become due hereunder are assignable.  5.   The   acceptance   of   payment   by   the   Contractor   shall   constitute   a   release   of   any  claims   for   liabilities   of   any   nature   whatsoever,   if   any,   owing   to   the   Contractor   from   the  Township , its agents, servants and employees.  6.   Contractor   agrees   to   indemnify   and   hold   harmless   the   Township,   its   agents,  servants   and   employees   from   and   against   any   losses,   damage,   liabilities   and   expenses   which   may  arise   or   be   claimed   against   the   Township,   its   agents,   servants   and   employees   consequent   upon   or  arising out of or in connection with the within Agreement.  7.   This   Agreement   shall   be   governed   and   construed   in   accordance   with   the   laws   of  the   State   of   New   Jersey,   without   regard   to   its   conflict   of   law   principles.   Any   claim   shall   be  brought   in   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Essex   Vicinage.   Each   of   the   Parties   hereby  irrevocably   consents   to   the   service   of   process   of   any   of   the   aforementioned   courts   in   any   such  action   or   proceeding   by   the   mailing   of   copies   thereof   to   it   by   regular   mail.   Each   of   the   Parties  hereby   irrevocably   waives   any   objection   that   it   may   no w   or   hereafter   have   to   the   laying   of   venue  of any such claim, suit, proceeding or action in such respective jurisdiction.  8.   This   Agreement   is   effective   January   1,   2026   and   covers   the   period   of   January   1,   2026  through   December   31,   2026.   The   Township   shall   have   the   option   to   extend   the   terms   of   this  Agreement   for   an   additional   one   (1)   year-term   from   January   1,   2027   through   December   31,  2027.  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF ,   the   parties   hereto   have   hereunto   set   their   hands   and   seals,   or  caused   the   presents   to   be   signed   by   their   proper   corporate   officers,   and   caused   these   corporate  seals to be affixed hereto.  ATTEST:   STERLING SECURITIES  _________________________________   ______________________________  Name   Name  ATTEST   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  _________________________________   BY:__________________________  KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE, R.M.C.   SUSAN McCARTNEY , MA YOR

28-26  Ja nuary 20, 2026  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION  GREEN ACRES PROGRAM  ENABLING RESOLUTION for SUPPLEMENTAL FUNDING REQUEST  WHEREAS, the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection, Green Acres Program  ( “ State ” ), provides loans and/or grants to municipal and county governments and grants to  nonprofit organizations for assistance in the acquisition and development of lands for outdoor  recreation and conservation purposes; and  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange has previously obtained a Green Acres loan of $0.00  and/or a Green Acres grant of   $ 6,0 00 ,000.00   from the State [and an Urban Parks grant of $0.00  from the State] to fund the following project(s)   pertaining to the entire 138-property referenced  as :  #0722-03-005- West Orange Township Open Space Acquisition   (Rock Spring Golf Course) ;  and  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange desires to further the public interest by requesting an  additional Green Acres loan of $0.00 and/or an additional Green Acres grant of   $ 3 , 75 0 ,000 .00   to  fund the project(s); and  WHEREAS, the State shall determine if the supplemental funding request is complete and in  conformance with the scope and intent of the Green Acres Program, and notify the applicant of  the amount of the funding award; and  WHEREAS, the applicant is willing to use the State ’ s funds in accordance with its rules,  regulations and applicable statutes, and is willing to enter into an Amendment of the Agreement  with the State for the above-named project;  NOW, THEREFORE, the governing body/board resolves that:  1. Susan McCartney or the successor to the office of Mayor is hereby authorized to:  (a) request such a loan and/or such a grant,  (b) provide additional information and furnish such documents as may be required, and  (c) act as the authorized correspondent of the above-named applicant; and  2. The applicant agrees to provide the local government/nonprofit share if a match is required.  3. The applicant agrees to comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws, rules, and  regulations in its performance of the project.  4. This resolution shall take effect immediately.

CERTIFICATION  I, Karen J. Carnevale R.M.C. Municipal Clerk do hereby certify that the foregoing is a true copy  of a resolution adopted by the Council of the   Township of West Orange at a meeting held on the  20 th   day of   January ,   202 6 .  IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunder set my hand and the official seal of this body this  20 th day of   January , 202 6 .  __________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale R.M.C.  Municipal Clerk

NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the West Orange Township Council that the Collector  of Taxes and Treasurer of West Orange, be and they are hereby authorized, empowered and directed to  cause to be paid to the homeowners the tax refunds due:  Karen J. documents.title: RESOLUTION - DEBT MANAGEMENT PLAN

pages.text: 30 -26  January 20, 2026  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   powers   include   the   authorization   to   issue   capital   and   bond  ordinances for the purposes of acquiring capital assets ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   seeks   to   achieve   and   maintain   bond   ratings   commensurate  with demonstrating a long-term commitment to sound financial planning ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   recognizes   that   annual   debt   service   costs   meet   or   exceed  12.50% of the operating budget; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   recognizes   that   not   actively   managing   capita l   needs   and   debt  issuance will have an adverse impact on the tax rate and the ability to provide services; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   memorialize   and   establish   many   of   the   existing  principles,   practices,   and   metrics   associated   with   the   management   of   its   existing   and   future   debt  portfolio ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   finds   and   determines   that   adoption   of   the   attached   Debt  Management   Policy   (the   “ Debt   Management   Policy ” )   will   help   ensure   that   the   debt   is   issued   and  managed prudently in order to mainta in sound fiscal policy; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   attached   policy   shall   serve   to   guide   the   decisions   and   actions   of   the  Township of West Orange ’ s existing and future debt related activities.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan Scarpa  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: January 20, 2026

Township of  West Orange  Debt & Finance  Presentation  Presented by:  John C. Ditinyak, CFO  Phoenix Advisors  December 9, 2025

The State of the Township’s Debt!  2  •   The Township’s net debt as of 2024 is over $171 million!  •   Net debt has increased by 56% over the last five years!  •   Payment of debt is 12.5% of the budget!  •   The Township, recognizing that allowing the debt to increase exponentially places a  financial burden on the residents, has developed a long-term debt management plan  that will guide this and future councils’ capital planning.

Debt Management Plan Goals:  3  •   Continue to pay down outstanding debt thereby reducing the related debt services  over time  •   Look to take advantage of lower interest rates, when possible, to permanently finance  outstanding notes, thereby reducing interest rate risk  •   Over time, replace borrowing with “pay-as-you-go” funding using cash rather than  credit for capital investment  •   Carefully manage capital spending with strategic planning to build “pay-go” capability  •   Achieve strategic success by freeing up millions of debt service dollars annually to  support operations, capital investment, and when possible, return value to the  taxpayers through increased usage of surplus rather than taxation.

What is Municipal Debt?  4  •   The Township has both Operating Expenses and Capital Expenses:  •   Operating Expenses include the salaries and wages of employees, utility expenses,  garbage and community programming - just to name a few. Operating Expenses  are broadly defined as the ongoing expenses to run the day-to-day Township  services.  •   Capital Expenses are individual purchases for land, infrastructure improvements,  equipment,   vehicles   and   major   renovation/construction   projects.   Capital  Expenses are typical high value purchases. For that reason, the cost is too great to  include the full amount of the purchase in one budget year.  •   For example, let’s say the Township wants to construct a new public works building.  The cost of the construction could be $30M. The Township will go out into the capital  markets and issue bonds for $30M so that we have the funds to pay for the project.  The bonds will then be repaid, with interest, via annual appropriations in the budget.  The repayment schedule follows the useful life of the asset that was purchased to  spread the cost over the generations of residents who benefit from the project. In this  example, the repayment would be over 25 years.

What is Municipal Debt?   (cont.)  5  •   Municipal debt can best be compared to a resident’s mortgage. Most homeowners do  not have the funds to pay cash for their home, so they go to a bank and get a long-  term loan (i.e. a mortgage). The homeowner pays principal and interest every month  until the loan is repaid.  •   The interest rate on the loan determines the amount of interest the homeowner will  pay over the life of the loan. For example, a 30-year mortgage for $500,000 at 5% will  cost the homeowner $465,000 in interest. If the rate moves up 1%, then the  homeowner will pay $580,000 in interest over the life of the loan.  •   Just as market conditions can raise or reduce interest rates for homeowners, the  Township’s short and long-term debt is impacted by changes in the municipal bond  market. As rates climb, the Township’s debt service expense will increase and will  generally be passed along to the taxpayer.  •   Due to the size and credit rating of municipal entities, municipal bond rates are  generally lower than those of a resident.   For example, a 30-year mortgage rate is  currently 6.25% and a 30-year tax-exempt municipal bond rate is currently 4.25%.
Historical Municipal Bond Rates  6

Current Debt Profile  7  •   The Township’s Statutory Net Debt is approximately $170 million. This includes:  •   $54 million of long-term, fixed rate bonds  •   $11.4 million of bond anticipation notes (“BANs”) coming due in March 2026  •   $62.7 million of BANs coming due in November 2026  •   $3.3 million of special emergency notes (“SENs”) coming due in November 2026  •   $38 million of authorized but not issued debt (includes JMEU authorizations)  •   $700k of NJIB loans (JMEU)  •   For 2025, debt service and deferred charges (SENs) combined for $13.5 million, or  12.5% of the total budget.  •   There are large drops in aggregate debt service in 2029 and 2038.  •   The Township’s current bond rating is “AA” (3 rd   highest) by S&P Global and was last  affirmed in October 2025. A bond rating is similar to a resident’s credit score, as it  impacts their cost to borrow.  •   The CFO and Phoenix review the outstanding debt annually to make sure the debt is  managed in the most cost-effective way possible given market conditions, future debt  authorizations, budgetary concerns and current capital needs.

Outstanding Bonded Debt Service by Series  8  (1) Excludes special assessment bonds, authorized but not issued debt, short-term debt (BANs) and deferred charges (SENs).  (2) Long-term bonds may be refinanced from time-to-time based on market conditions and redemption provisions.

Future Debt & Capital Discussion  9  •   The Township Council and CFO have expressed a desire to begin to transition to an  annual pay-go capital plan funded by the budget in lieu of borrowing short and long-  term debt.  •   The Township expects to receive $6.3 million in green acres grants (Rock Spring) and  $10.8 million from the sale of land (55 Lakeside). These funds will be used towards the  repayment of debt.  •   The CFO has identified approximately $22.8 million of authorized but not issued bond  ordinances that will also need to be funded over the next two (2) years, as the projects  are ongoing and will require cash to fund them.  •   This excludes approximately $15 million of JMEU authorizations that may not  need to be funded by the Township.  •   Based on the Township’s outstanding long-term debt, a bond issuance between 2026-  2028 would be the best time to permanently finance the debt, assuming favorable  market conditions.

Proposed Debt & Capital Plan  10  •   The proposed debt plan targets an annual pay-go capital program of $5.5 million per  year ($5 million capital and $500k special emergencies), which is expected to be 100%  funded by the budget by 2038 with 2% annual increases to debt service in the interim.  •   For 2025, we included $6.7 million of new debt authorization (ordinance 2915-25) and  $800,000 of special emergencies.  •   From 2026 to 2036, new annual capital needs of $5.5 million would be funded by a mix  of debt authorizations, special emergencies and pay-go capital.  •   In addition, approximately $22.8 million of authorized but not issued bond ordinances  will be funded over the next two (2) years by debt issuance.  •   The proposed future debt issuances include:  •   Roll existing notes to 2028  •   Issue new notes in 2025-2036 to finance "Expected New Debt"  •   Issue $81.4 million bonds between 2026-2028 to finance all outstanding notes  •   Roll new notes to 2037  •   Issue $14.6 million bonds in 2037 to finance all outstanding notes  •   Roll SENs with mandatory pay downs and annual new money as shown above  •   Full migration to pay-go capital funding beginning 2038

Aggregate Debt & Capital Projections   (cont.)  12  (1) Includes all Current Fund debt including: general improvement bonds, NJIB loans, debt service on BANs for 2025 and deferred charges (SENs) for 2025.  Excludes special assessment bonds debt service.  (2) Rates assumed to increase 0.25% for bonds and notes each year. Includes voluntary principal pay downs on notes that are subject to change.  (3) Annual pay-go capital funded as a combination of debt and cash as described herein.

Financial Debt Metrics  14  •   Net   debt   is   limited   to   3.5%   of   the  equalized valuation basis per State statute.  •   As of 2024, net debt was $171 million or  2.2%.  •   Net debt has increased by 56% over the  last five (5) years.  •   Debt service is typically +/- 10% of the  municipal budget for NJ municipalities.  •   In   2025,   the   Township’s   debt   service  (including   deferred   charges/SENs)   was  12.5% of budget.  •   Annual debt service has increased from $8  million in 2020 to $13.5 million in 2025.

2948-26  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Th is   Ordinance, which amends the Traffic Regulations of the Township, amends the  Parking Regulations for Washington Street , east of Watchung Avenue,   to   create   a   15-minute  parking   zone   for   the   business   located at 208 Watchung Avenue.   This action is in response to   a  request for more turnover in the available spaces   in the area , which   on Watchung Avenue   tend to  be taken by nearby residents or business   patrons   who park all day. The time limited parking in  this   are a will be in effect   daily   from   5 :00 AM to   8 :00 PM.   There is no overnight parking in this  area between 2 A.M. and 5 A.M.   The Parking Regulations for Washington Street west of  Watchung Avenue were previously updated in 2024.

2949-26  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends   Chapter 7-37.1,   the Regulation for the Movement and Parking of Traffic  on Municipal and Board of Education Property, to   add new sections to the ordinance regulating  overnight parking by residents   in Non-Metered or Non-Permitted M unicipal Properties .   Additionally, this  ordinance will prohibit parking when snow covered to facilitate plowing operations.  Specifically,   this   amendment   targets   use   of   the   Township ’ s   Dog   Park   parking   lot ,   but   can   be  tailored for future regulation of additional Municipal Properties.

Pursuant   to   N.J.S.A   39:4-197.6,   Restricted   Parking   Zones,   as   defined   in  N.J.S.A.   39:4-207.6e,   are   established   in   front   of   the   following   residences   as   indicated   on   the   Tax   Maps   of  the   Township   of   West   Orange,   at   the   specified   locations.   Parking   within   a   specific   zone   is   restricted   to  the   motor   vehicle   issued   a   permit   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   39:4-197.7.   In   accordance   with   N.J.S.A.,   39:4-  197.6   parking   is   permitted   in   the   Restricted   Parking   Zone   except   wh en   prohibited   in   front   of   the  residence by other sections of this Chapter.  Residence Address   No. of Spaces   Location  160 South V alley Road   1   East   side.   Beginning   153   feet   north   of  the northerly curbline of Meeker Street  extended, and extending 20 feet north  1 48 Chestnut Street   1   Beginning   132   feet   east   of the  east erly curb   line of   Harrison Avenue  extended, and extending 20  feet   east .  Section   2 .   If any provision of this Ordinance o r application thereof, under any  circumstances,   is   held   invalid,   the   invalidity   shall   not   affect   any   other   provisions   or   applications   of   this  Ordinance   that   can   be   given   effect   without   the   invalid   provision(s)   or   application(s)   and   to   this   end   the  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   are   severable.   All   other   Ordinances   or   parts   of   Ordinances   inconsistent   with  this Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section   3.   All   other   ordinances   in   conflict   or   inconsistent   with   this   ordinance   are   hereby  repealed,   to   the   extent   of   su ch   conflict   or   inconsistency.   In   the   event   of   any   inconsistencies   between   the

AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER  www.westorange.org  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   and   any   prior   ordinance   of   the   Township ,   the   provisions   are   hereof   shall   be  determined   to   govern.   All   other   parts,   portions,   and   provisions   of   the   Ordinances   of   the   Township   are  hereby ratified and confirmed, except where inconsistent with the terms hereof.  Section 4.   This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in  accordance with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Dep artment of Transportation if required.  SUSAN MCCARTNEY, MAYOR  SUSAN SCARPA,   COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.  MUNICIPAL CLERK  INTRODUCED: January   20 , 2026  ADOPTED:   February 10 , 2026

AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER  www.westorange.org  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  A   Restricted   Parking   Stall   will   be   established   in   front   of   1 48   Chestnut   Street   for   the   exclusive   use  of   the   resident   who   is   in   possession   of   a   NJMVC   Disability   ID   and   Parking   Placard.   Th is   Stall   w as  requeste d   by   the   resident   and   is   permissible   under   NJ   Statutes   Annotated   (N.J.S.A.)   39:4-197.7.   Parking  is   permitted   in   the   restricted   parking   zone   except   when   prohibited   by   other   sections   of   Chapter   7   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange.   Additionally,   a   Restricted   Parking   Stall   at  160   South   Valley   Road   will   be   eliminated   at   the   request   of   the   permittee ’ s   family   because   it   is   no   longer  needed.

2951-26  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECT I ON S 7-7.3 TEMPORARY  PARKING   PROHIBITION   FOR   SNOW PLOWING AND REMOVAL,   AND   7- 14. 1c NIGHT PARKING RESTRICTED ,   OF  THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of Essex,  State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-7.3 Temporary Parking Prohibition for Snow Plowing and Removal, of the  Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as  follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Name of Street   Side   Days   Location  Kenz   Terrace   Both   All   Entire Length  Section 2.   Subsection 7-14.1c Night Par king Restricted,   of the Revised General Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold  type denotes addition):  EXCEPTIONS  Name of Street   Side   Location  Kenz   Terrace   Both   Entire Length  Section 3.   All   other   ordinances   in   conflict   or   inconsistent   with   this   ordinance   are   hereby  repealed,   to   the   extent   of   such   conflict   or   inconsistency.   In   the   event   of   any   inconsistencies   between   the  provisions   of   this   Ordinance   an d   any   prior   ordinance   of   the   Township ,   the   provisions   are   hereof   shall   be  determined   to   govern.   All   other   parts,   portions,   and   provisions   of   the   Ordinances   of   the   Township   are   hereby  ratified and confirmed, except where inconsistent with the terms hereof.  Section   4 .   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any circumstances, is  held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this Ordinance that can be  given effect without the valid provision(s ) or application and to this end, the provisions of this Ordinance are  severable.  All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby repealed.
SUSAN MCCARTNEY, MAYOR  SUSAN SCARPA,   COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.  MUNICIPAL CLERK  INTRODUCED: January   20 , 2026  ADOPTED:   February 10 , 2026

2951-26  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance   creates an opportunity for overnight parking on   Kenz   Terrace, where residents   have  requested   on - street parking in addition to their private driveways. In addition, the ordinance requires the  residents to move those vehicles as needed for the purpose of snow plowing .

#95826025.1 168182.012  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  IN THE   COUNTY OF   ESSEX ,  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  CAPITAL ORDINANCE NUMBER   2952-26  CAPITAL   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR   THE   COST   OF  PRELIMINARY   ENGINEERING   EXPENSES   IN   CONNECTION  WITH   THE   GREGORY   AVENUE   AND   LOWELL   AVENUE  SAFE   ROUTES   TO   SCHOOL   PROJECT ,   BY   AND   IN   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF  ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY ;   APPROPRIATING  $ 387,047.59   THEREFOR   TO   PAY   FOR   THE   COST   THEREOF  (WHICH   AMOUNT   WILL   BE   FUNDED   BY   A   GRANT   IN   THE  AMOUNT   OF   $ 387,047.59   RECEIVED   OR   EXPECTED   TO   BE  RECEIVED   FROM   THE   NEW   JERSEY   DEPARTMENT   OF  TRANSPORTATION )  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   AND   ENACTED   BY   THE  TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF  ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY   (a   majority   of   the   full   membership   thereof   affirmatively  concurring), AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION 1.   The   capital   improvement   and   purpose   described   in   Section  2   of   this   capital   ordinance   is   hereby   authorized   as   a   general   capital   improvement   and  purpose   to   be   undertaken   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex ,   State  of   New   Jersey   (the   “ Township ” ).   For   the   improvement   and   purpose   stated   in   Section   2  hereof,   there   is   hereby   appropriated   the   principal   amount   of   $ 387,047.59 ,   which   amount   will  be   funded   by   a   grant   received   or   expected   to   be   received   from   the   New   Jersey   Department  of   Transportation   (the   “ Grant ” )   and   deposited   in   the   Capital   Improvement   Fund   or   the   Grant  Fund of the   Township .

#95826025.1 168182.012  SECTION 2.   ( a )   The   improvement   and   purpose   hereby   authorized   is   for  the   cost   of   p reliminary   e ngineer ing   expenses   in   connection   with   the   Gregory   Avenue   and  Lowell   Avenue   Safe   Routes   to   School   Project ,   a   federally   funded   program   designed   to  increase   pedestrian   safety   near   schools   to   include ,   among   other   things,   new   sidewalks,  high-visibility   crosswalks,   ADA   curb   ramps ,   LED   stop   signs   and   flashing   beacons   along  Gregory Avenue and Lowell Avenue .  ( b )   The   improvement   and   purpose   set   forth   in   Section   2(a)   above   shall   also  include,   as   applicable,   all   engineering ,   design   and   site   work,   surveying,   construction  management   services,   construction   planning,   preparation   of   plans   and   specifications,  permits,   bid   documents,   construction   inspection   and   contract   administration,   environmental  testing   and   remediation,   and   also   all   work,   tools,   materials,   equipment,   labor   and  appurtenances as necessary therefor or incidental thereto.  SECTION 3.   The   expenditure   of   the   Grant   for   the   improvement   and  purpose   set forth in Section 2 (a)   hereof   is   hereby authorized and approved.  SECTION 4.   The   Township   hereby   certifies   that   it   has   adopted   a   capital  budget   or   a   temporary   capital   budget   of   the   Township ,   as   applicable .   The   capital   budget   or  temporary   capital   budget ,   as   applicable,   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   conform  with   the   provisions   of   this   capital   ordinance   to   the   extent   of   any   inconsistency   herewith.   To  the   extent   that   the   improvement   and   purpose   authorized   herein   is   inconsistent   with   the  adopted   capital   budget   or   temporary   capital   budget   of   the   Township ,   a   revised   capital  budget   or   temporary   capital   budget   of   the   Township   has   been   filed   with   the   Division   of   Local  Government Services ,   in the Department of Community Affairs of the State of New Jersey .

#95826025.1 168182.012  SECTION 5.   This   capital   ordinance   shall   take   effect   immediately   after  final adoption as described in   N.J.S.A.   40:49-2 and   40: 69A-181 .  ADOPTED ON FIRST READING  DATED:   January 20, 2026  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, Clerk   SUSAN SCARPA ,   Council President  Township of West Orange   Township of West Orange  ADOPTED ON SECOND READING  DATED:   February   10 , 2026  KAREN J. #95826025.1 168182.012  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  IN THE COUNTY OF   ESSEX ,  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  CLERK'S CERTIFICATE  I,   KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE ,   DO   HEREBY   CERTIFY   that   I   am   the   Clerk   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   (the   “ Township ” ),   a   municipal   corporation  organized   and   existing   under   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and   that ,   as   such ,   I   am  duly   authorized   to   execute   and   deliver   this   certificate   on   behalf   of   the   Township . The   representations   made   herein   are   based   upon   th e   records   of   the   Township . ( 2 )   After   introduction,   the   capital   ordinance   was   published   as   required  by applicable law on   January   22 ,   202 6   in   the West Oran ge Chronicle .  ( 3 )   Following   the   passage   of   the   capital   ordinance   on   first   reading,  and   at   least   seven   (7)   days   prior   to   the   final   adoption   thereof,   I   caused   to   be   posted   in   the  principal   municipal   building   of   the   Township   at   the   place   where   public   notices   are  customarily   posted,   a   copy   of   said   capital   ordinance   or   a   summary   thereof   and   a   notice   that  copies   of   the   capital   ordinance   would   be   made   available   to   the   members   of   the   general  public   of   the   Township   who   requested   copies,   up   to   and   including   the   time   of   further  conside ration   of   the   capital   ordinance   by   the   governing   body   of   the   Township .   Copies   of  the capital ordinance were made available to all who requested same .

#95826025.1 168182.012  ( 4 )   After   final   passage   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township ,   the  capital   ordinance   was   duly   approved   by   the   Mayor   of   the   Township   and   duly   published   as  required by law   on   February   12 , 202 6   in   the West Orange Ch ron icle .  ( 5 )   The   capital   ordinance   has   not   been   amended,   added   to,   altered   or  repealed and said capital ordinance is now in full force and effect.  ( 6 )   A   certified   copy   of   the   capital   ordinance   and   a   copy   of   the  amended   capital   budget   form   or   temporary   capital   budget   form ,   if   applicable,   have   been  filed   with   the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local   Government   Services ,   in   the   Department   of  Community Affairs of the State of New Jersey .  ( 7 )   An   impression   of   the   official   seal   of   the   Township   is   affixed,  imprinted or reproduced opposite my signature on this Certificate .  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   I   have   hereunto   set   my   hand   and   affixed ,   imprinted  or reproduced   the official seal of the   Township   as of   this   13 th   day of   February ,   202 6 .  (SEAL)  KAREN J. CARNEVALE ,  Clerk of the   Township of West Orange

pages.text: 30 -26  February 10 , 2026  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   powers   include   the   authorization   to   issue   capital   and   bond  ordinances for the purposes of acquiring capital assets ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   seeks   to   achieve   and   maintain   bond   ratings   commensurate  with demonstrating a long-term commitment to sound financial planning ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   recognizes   that   annual   debt   service   costs   meet   or   exceed  12.50% of the operating budget; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   recognizes   that   not   actively   managing   capital   needs   and   de bt  issuance will have an adverse impact on the tax rate and the ability to provide services; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   memorialize   and   establish   many   of   the   existing  principles,   practices,   and   metrics   associated   with   the   management   of   its   existing   and   future   debt  portfolio ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   finds   and   determines   that   adoption   of   the   attached   Debt  Management   Policy   (the   “ Debt   Management   Policy ” )   will   help   ensure   that   the   debt   is   issued   and  managed prudently in order to maintain sound fisca l policy; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   attached   policy   shall   serve   to   guide   the   decisions   and   actions   of   the  Township of West Orange ’ s existing and future debt related activities.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan Scarpa  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   Febr uary 10 , 2026

Township of  West Orange  Debt & Finance  Presentation  Presented by:  John C. Ditinyak, CFO  Phoenix Advisors  December 9, 2025

The State of the Township’s Debt!  2  •   The Township’s net debt as of 2024 is over $171 million!  •   Net debt has increased by 56% over the last five years!  •   Payment of debt is 12.5% of the budget!  •   The Township, recognizing that allowing the debt to increase exponentially places a  financial burden on the residents, has developed a long-term debt management plan  that will guide this and future councils’ capital planning.

Debt Management Plan Goals:  3  •   Continue to pay down outstanding debt thereby reducing the related debt services  over time  •   Look to take advantage of lower interest rates, when possible, to permanently finance  outstanding notes, thereby reducing interest rate risk  •   Over time, replace borrowing with “pay-as-you-go” funding using cash rather than  credit for capital investment  •   Carefully manage capital spending with strategic planning to build “pay-go” capability  •   Achieve strategic success by freeing up millions of debt service dollars annually to  support operations, capital investment, and when possible, return value to the  taxpayers through increased usage of surplus rather than taxation.

What is Municipal Debt?  4  •   The Township has both Operating Expenses and Capital Expenses:  •   Operating Expenses include the salaries and wages of employees, utility expenses,  garbage and community programming - just to name a few. Operating Expenses  are broadly defined as the ongoing expenses to run the day-to-day Township  services.  •   Capital Expenses are individual purchases for land, infrastructure improvements,  equipment,   vehicles   and   major   renovation/construction   projects.   Capital  Expenses are typical high value purchases. For that reason, the cost is too great to  include the full amount of the purchase in one budget year.  •   For example, let’s say the Township wants to construct a new public works building.  The cost of the construction could be $30M. The Township will go out into the capital  markets and issue bonds for $30M so that we have the funds to pay for the project.  The bonds will then be repaid, with interest, via annual appropriations in the budget.  The repayment schedule follows the useful life of the asset that was purchased to  spread the cost over the generations of residents who benefit from the project. In this  example, the repayment would be over 25 years.

What is Municipal Debt?   (cont.)  5  •   Municipal debt can best be compared to a resident’s mortgage. Most homeowners do  not have the funds to pay cash for their home, so they go to a bank and get a long-  term loan (i.e. a mortgage). The homeowner pays principal and interest every month  until the loan is repaid.  •   The interest rate on the loan determines the amount of interest the homeowner will  pay over the life of the loan. For example, a 30-year mortgage for $500,000 at 5% will  cost the homeowner $465,000 in interest. If the rate moves up 1%, then the  homeowner will pay $580,000 in interest over the life of the loan.  •   Just as market conditions can raise or reduce interest rates for homeowners, the  Township’s short and long-term debt is impacted by changes in the municipal bond  market. As rates climb, the Township’s debt service expense will increase and will  generally be passed along to the taxpayer.  •   Due to the size and credit rating of municipal entities, municipal bond rates are  generally lower than those of a resident.   For example, a 30-year mortgage rate is  currently 6.25% and a 30-year tax-exempt municipal bond rate is currently 4.25%.
Historical Municipal Bond Rates  6

Current Debt Profile  7  •   The Township’s Statutory Net Debt is approximately $170 million. This includes:  •   $54 million of long-term, fixed rate bonds  •   $11.4 million of bond anticipation notes (“BANs”) coming due in March 2026  •   $62.7 million of BANs coming due in November 2026  •   $3.3 million of special emergency notes (“SENs”) coming due in November 2026  •   $38 million of authorized but not issued debt (includes JMEU authorizations)  •   $700k of NJIB loans (JMEU)  •   For 2025, debt service and deferred charges (SENs) combined for $13.5 million, or  12.5% of the total budget.  •   There are large drops in aggregate debt service in 2029 and 2038.  •   The Township’s current bond rating is “AA” (3 rd   highest) by S&P Global and was last  affirmed in October 2025. A bond rating is similar to a resident’s credit score, as it  impacts their cost to borrow.  •   The CFO and Phoenix review the outstanding debt annually to make sure the debt is  managed in the most cost-effective way possible given market conditions, future debt  authorizations, budgetary concerns and current capital needs.

Outstanding Bonded Debt Service by Series  8  (1) Excludes special assessment bonds, authorized but not issued debt, short-term debt (BANs) and deferred charges (SENs).  (2) Long-term bonds may be refinanced from time-to-time based on market conditions and redemption provisions.

Future Debt & Capital Discussion  9  •   The Township Council and CFO have expressed a desire to begin to transition to an  annual pay-go capital plan funded by the budget in lieu of borrowing short and long-  term debt.  •   The Township expects to receive $6.3 million in green acres grants (Rock Spring) and  $10.8 million from the sale of land (55 Lakeside). These funds will be used towards the  repayment of debt.  •   The CFO has identified approximately $22.8 million of authorized but not issued bond  ordinances that will also need to be funded over the next two (2) years, as the projects  are ongoing and will require cash to fund them.  •   This excludes approximately $15 million of JMEU authorizations that may not  need to be funded by the Township.  •   Based on the Township’s outstanding long-term debt, a bond issuance between 2026-  2028 would be the best time to permanently finance the debt, assuming favorable  market conditions.

Proposed Debt & Capital Plan  10  •   The proposed debt plan targets an annual pay-go capital program of $5.5 million per  year ($5 million capital and $500k special emergencies), which is expected to be 100%  funded by the budget by 2038 with 2% annual increases to debt service in the interim.  •   For 2025, we included $6.7 million of new debt authorization (ordinance 2915-25) and  $800,000 of special emergencies.  •   From 2026 to 2036, new annual capital needs of $5.5 million would be funded by a mix  of debt authorizations, special emergencies and pay-go capital.  •   In addition, approximately $22.8 million of authorized but not issued bond ordinances  will be funded over the next two (2) years by debt issuance.  •   The proposed future debt issuances include:  •   Roll existing notes to 2028  •   Issue new notes in 2025-2036 to finance "Expected New Debt"  •   Issue $81.4 million bonds between 2026-2028 to finance all outstanding notes  •   Roll new notes to 2037  •   Issue $14.6 million bonds in 2037 to finance all outstanding notes  •   Roll SENs with mandatory pay downs and annual new money as shown above  •   Full migration to pay-go capital funding beginning 2038

Aggregate Debt & Capital Projections   (cont.)  12  (1) Includes all Current Fund debt including: general improvement bonds, NJIB loans, debt service on BANs for 2025 and deferred charges (SENs) for 2025.  Excludes special assessment bonds debt service.  (2) Rates assumed to increase 0.25% for bonds and notes each year. Includes voluntary principal pay downs on notes that are subject to change.  (3) Annual pay-go capital funded as a combination of debt and cash as described herein.

Financial Debt Metrics  14  •   Net   debt   is   limited   to   3.5%   of   the  equalized valuation basis per State statute.  •   As of 2024, net debt was $171 million or  2.2%.  •   Net debt has increased by 56% over the  last five (5) years.  •   Debt service is typically +/- 10% of the  municipal budget for NJ municipalities.  •   In   2025,   the   Township’s   debt   service  (including   deferred   charges/SENs)   was  12.5% of budget.  •   Annual debt service has increased from $8  million in 2020 to $13.5 million in 2025.

3 2 -26  February 10 , 2026  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the  exclusion of the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters;  and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township  Council it is required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject  to be discussed and stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussi on  conducted in closed session   can be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of  N.J.S.A. 10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be  it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now  assembled in public session   at   its regular meeting of   February 10 , 2026   enter into a closed  session to consider the following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an   i nvasion of  individual privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public  provided that their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of  New Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or  maybe a party.  ( X )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that  confidentiality is   r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  ( )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State  statute or rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right  to receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ()   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real  property with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public   interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.

( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a  civil penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.  More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  Discussion on Governmental Structure , including   but not limited to, Personnel, Legal  Matters, Contract Negotiations, Property Transactions and Statutory / Regula tory matte rs .  (the general subject matter ( s )   of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate  box and shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can  be made without compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R   RESOLVED   that   the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the  public interest will no longer   be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing   r esolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange at a public meeting held   on   February 10 , 2026 . 49-26  Febru ary 10, 2026  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING CONTRACTS WITH CERTAIN APPROVED   COOPERTIVE  VENDORS FOR CONTRACTING UNITS PURSUANT TO   N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5(2)   FOR 202 6  Whereas ,   pursuant to the provision made in the Local Public Contracts Law N.J.S.A.  40A:1 0 & 40A:1 1-5(2) the Township as a Local Government Unit, may without public  advertising for goods or services enter into contracts already secured by the United States of  America, the State of New Jersey, County or Municipality; and  Whereas , the Township of West Orange has the need on a timely basis to purchase goods  or services utilizing   Cooperative Contracts ; and  Whereas , the Township of West Orange intends to enter into contracts with the attached  Referenced   Cooperative   Contract Vendors through this resolution and properly executed  contracts, which shall be subject to all the conditions applicable to the current   Cooperative  contracts;  Now, Therefore, Be It Resolved , that the Township of West Orange   Council   authorizes  the Purchasing Agent to purchase certain goods or services from those approved   Cooperative  Contract Vendors on the attached list, pursuant to all conditions of the individual   Cooperative  contracts ; and  Be It Further Resolved , that the governing body of the Township of West Orange  pursuant to N.J.A.C. 5:30-5.5(b), the certification of available funds, shall either certify the full  maximum amount against the budget at the time the contract is awarded, or no contract amount  shall be chargeable or certified until such time as the goods or services are ordered or otherwise  called for prior to placing the order, and a certification of availability of funds is made by the  Chief Finance Officer; and  Be It Further Resolved , that the duration of the contracts between the Township of West  Orange and the Referenced State Contract Vendors shall be   through   December 31, 202 6   or when  they end whichever comes sooner .  Karen J. Carnevale,   R.M.C.,   Municipal Clerk   Susan Scarpa , Council   President  Adopted: February 10, 2026  I hereby certify funds available   from V arious Account s:  John C. Ditinyak , Chief Financial Officer
